vulnerability.reason: Both describe a lack of verification (audits/redundant checks) leading to trust in potentially erroneous automated output.
vulnerability.reference: Reliance on black-box RL optimization in production settings without real-time process audits propagates unseen errors.
vulnerability.generated: Engineers lack redundant measurement methods and real-time calibration checks, so they trust automated NMR output that may mischaracterize real low-field spin relaxation.

impact.reason: Affected party (alloy batches) present, but damage type only partially matches: structural failures similar to mechanical failures, but recalls and reputational damage are missing; scrap and delays are different.
impact.reference: Faulty alloy batches lead to mechanical failures, recalls, and reputational damage.
impact.generated: Non-conforming alloy batches are produced, causing material scrap, production delays, and potential structural failures in end-use components.

dose_response.reason: The generated arc includes mischaracterization of relaxation and trust without verification, but omits the intermediate step of miscalibrated pulse modulation, altering the causal mechanism.
dose_response.reference: RL-trained pulse sequences mis-characterize spin relaxation, enabling miscalibrated pulse modulation, which when trusted without real-time audits by QC engineers, leads to faulty batches causing mechanical failures, recalls, and reputational damage.
dose_response.generated: RL-trained pulse sequences that mis-characterize spin relaxation under low-field conditions, through QC engineers using automated NMR, triggers their lack of redundant calibration, causing trust in inaccurate output and leading to non-conforming alloy batches, scrap, delays, and structural failures.
